title: olearia glutescens
intro: Olearia glutescens, also known as tree daisy or joint bush, is a shrubby perennial plant native to southeastern Australia, recognized by its white or pink flowers and grey-green leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its rich composition of flavonoids and terpenoids. The medicinal properties of Olearia glutescens have been found to provide various health benefits, including pain relief, wound healing, and the management of gastrointestinal issues. Olearia glutescens can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, although its use should be cautioned against due to potential interactions with other medications and allergic reactions in some individuals.
